name: responsive
context: Tailwind responsive design — breakpoints, mobile-first, max-width variants, container queries, and custom breakpoints
info: Tailwind uses a mobile-first breakpoint system where unprefixed utilities apply to all screen sizes and prefixed utilities apply at that breakpoint and above. The default breakpoints are sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px), and 2xl (1536px). Agents must understand mobile-first to avoid the common mistake of using sm: for mobile styles — sm: means 640px and up, not small screens. v4 adds built-in container queries (@container) and max-width variants (max-sm:, max-md:) as first-class features. This node teaches agents how to build responsive layouts, use breakpoint ranges, customize breakpoints via @theme, and leverage container queries for component-level responsiveness.
triggers: what does sm: mean in tailwind
hide on mobile show on desktop tailwind
body:
# Responsive Design in Tailwind Tailwind is mobile-first: unprefixed utilities apply to all sizes, prefixed utilities apply at that breakpoint **and above**. ## Default Breakpoints | Prefix | Min Width | CSS | Common devices | |--------|-----------|-----|----------------| | *(none)* | 0px | (no media query) | All screens | | `sm:` | 640px (40rem) | `@media (width >= 40rem)` | Large phones landscape | | `md:` | 768px (48rem) | `@media (width >= 48rem)` | Tablets | | `lg:` | 1024px (64rem) | `@media (width >= 64rem)` | Laptops | | `xl:` | 1280px (80rem) | `@media (width >= 80rem)` | Desktops | | `2xl:` | 1536px (96rem) | `@media (width >= 96rem)` | Large monitors | ## Mobile-First Pattern **Always design for mobile first, then add breakpoints for larger screens.** ```html
Left
Right

Wrong approach

Right approach

``` ## Common Responsive Patterns ### Responsive Grid ```html

``` ### Show/Hide by Screen Size ```html

``` ### Responsive Spacing ```html
Content with responsive padding and max-width
``` ## Max-Width Variants (v4) Apply styles *below* a breakpoint: ```html
Item
Item
``` | Variant | Media Query | |---------|-------------| | `max-sm:` | `@media (width < 40rem)` | | `max-md:` | `@media (width < 48rem)` | | `max-lg:` | `@media (width < 64rem)` | | `max-xl:` | `@media (width < 80rem)` | | `max-2xl:` | `@media (width < 96rem)` | ### Target a Single Breakpoint Range ```html
Centered only on tablets
``` ## Container Queries (v4) Style based on **parent container size** instead of viewport: ```html
Responds to container width, not viewport
``` ### Default Container Sizes | Variant | Min Width | |---------|-----------| | `@3xs:` | 256px (16rem) | | `@2xs:` | 288px (18rem) | | `@xs:` | 320px (20rem) | | `@sm:` | 384px (24rem) | | `@md:` | 448px (28rem) | | `@lg:` | 512px (32rem) | | `@xl:` | 576px (36rem) | | `@2xl:` | 672px (42rem) | | `@7xl:` | 1280px (80rem) | ### Named Containers ```html
Responds to the sidebar container specifically
``` ## Custom Breakpoints ### v4 (CSS @theme) ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --breakpoint-xs: 30rem; /* Add new breakpoint */ --breakpoint-3xl: 120rem; /* Add new breakpoint */ --breakpoint-sm: 36rem; /* Override existing */ } ``` ### Replace All Breakpoints ```css @theme { --breakpoint-*: initial; /* Remove defaults */ --breakpoint-tablet: 40rem; --breakpoint-laptop: 64rem; --breakpoint-desktop: 80rem; } ``` Use as: `tablet:flex-row`, `laptop:grid-cols-3` ### Arbitrary Breakpoints (One-Off) ```html
One-off breakpoint without theme configuration
``` ## Viewport Meta Tag Always include in `` for responsive design to work: ```html ```

sections.title: Default breakpoint values
sections.body: sm: 640px (40rem), md: 768px (48rem), lg: 1024px (64rem), xl: 1280px (80rem), 2xl: 1536px (96rem). These are min-width breakpoints — `md:flex` means `@media (width >= 48rem) { display: flex }`. Unprefixed utilities have no media query and apply at all sizes.

sections.title: Mobile-first methodology
sections.body: Design for mobile first with unprefixed classes, then progressively enhance for larger screens with breakpoint prefixes. Common mistake: using `sm:` for mobile styles — `sm:` is 640px+, not small screens. The mobile style is the unprefixed version. Example: `text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg` starts small and grows.

sections.title: Max-width and range variants
sections.body: v4 adds `max-sm:`, `max-md:`, `max-lg:`, `max-xl:`, `max-2xl:` for styles below a breakpoint. Combine for ranges: `md:max-lg:text-center` applies only between 768px and 1024px. Useful for mobile-only overrides without restructuring the mobile-first cascade.

sections.title: Container queries
sections.body: Add `@container` to a parent element. Children use `@sm:`, `@md:`, `@lg:` etc. to style based on container width instead of viewport. Named containers with `@container/name` and `@sm/name:` target specific ancestors. Custom sizes via `@theme { --container-8xl: 96rem; }`. Container query units available: `w-[50cqw]` for 50% of container width. Container queries are built-in to v4 — no plugin required.

sections.title: Customizing breakpoints
sections.body: v4: add `--breakpoint-name: value;` in `@theme`. Use `--breakpoint-*: initial;` to remove all defaults. v3: set in `theme.screens` of `tailwind.config.js`. Arbitrary one-off breakpoints: `min-[320px]:text-center`, `max-[600px]:hidden`. Named breakpoints generate prefixed variants automatically.
